headline: Rain through Monday; dry Tuesday for garden work
weather_notes: Tonight 86–90% chance of thunderstorms, low 39°F — beds get natural water; skip all irrigation through Monday.
Monday stays wet (75% precip, high 56°F) — cold saturated soil will slow root establishment in week-old tomato transplants (e1–e4).
Monday night drops to 37°F — near-freezing temperatures for 8-day tomatoes will inhibit root activity.
Tuesday clears to 64°F with near-zero precip — first dry window after two wet days, ideal for post-rain inspections.
Wednesday reaches 72°F and sunny — the week's best outdoor working conditions for any pending tasks.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
surfaced_tasks.reason: Two days of rain ending Monday create ideal blight-spreading conditions; inspect lower leaves during Tuesday's clear window, right before the May 20 due date.

suggested_tasks.title: Check w3 Italian Red for garlic scapes
suggested_tasks.description: Late May is peak scape time for hardneck garlic in Zone 7b — remove scapes promptly now to redirect energy to bulb development before the July harvest.
